c1.speaker: warren
c1.line: You've heard, then. That I doubted him.

c2.speaker: narrator
c2.line: He takes off his glasses and cleans them, slowly. They were clean already.

press.line: Did you want to keep the chair?

empathize.line: Eight years is a long time to keep someone else's seat warm.

observe.line: You've cleaned those glasses three times since I said his name.

c4pb.speaker: narrator
c4pb.line: True. Closed. The room gets one degree colder — the fact will have to come back through a longer door.

c4o.speaker: warren
c4o.line: ...You're Archie's, all right. Sit down, Mr. Gibbons.

c4.speaker: warren
c4.line: I did. I do. That is not the same as wanting his chair, Mr. Gibbons. Here. My speech. Read the third draft. I couldn't get the joy right in the first two.

c5.speaker: gary
c5.line: "...the happiest sentence I will ever say: the waiting is over."

c6.speaker: warren
c6.line: Eight years I've kept a seat warm for someone else. Do you know what I've kept cold? Forty-seven of them. I intend to photograph every one before my knees give out. I wasn't afraid of losing the chair. I was afraid of the boy losing himself in it.
